title: ipomoea tolmerana subsp. occidentalis
intro: Ipomoea tolmerana subsp. occidentalis, also known as the western Ipomoea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially alleviating symptoms of fever and pain. Herbal preparations of Ipomoea tolmerana subsp. occidentalis can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
